summary: The diff implements the vast majority of requirements for the Workshop Cashier POS application. All eight requirements are substantially addressed: backend Motoko actor, shop settings, catalog management, cashier/transaction, receipt printing, transaction history, reports, and visual theme. A few specific acceptance criteria appear partially or not fully implemented based on the available diff evidence.

missing_requirements.requirement: Editing a product/service should update name and price in the backend (not just stock). The CatalogPage edit flow for products only calls updateProductStock, and there is no mutation for updating a product's name or price.
missing_requirements.reason: The CatalogPage diff shows that when editing a product, only updateStock.mutateAsync is called (updating stock only). No backend function or frontend mutation for updating a product's name/price is visible. Similarly, no updateService mutation is shown for services.

missing_requirements.requirement: Top-selling items report should be filterable by the selected period (date/month), not just a global top-N list.
missing_requirements.reason: The ReportsPage calls useTopSellingItems(BigInt(10)) with only a count parameter, with no date/period filter passed. The requirement states top-selling items should be for the selected period.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend exposes queries for daily and monthly sales totals and transaction counts — the backend main.mo is truncated so daily/monthly aggregation query implementations cannot be fully confirmed.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend file is truncated after product/service management. Daily and monthly report query functions cannot be verified from the diff alone.

hallucinated_features.description: Customer management: useCustomers and useAddCustomer hooks, and a customer suggestions/autocomplete feature in the Cashier page.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention a customer database, customer list queries, or autocomplete for returning customers. The backend type definitions and CashierPage import these features without any spec basis.

hallucinated_features.description: Delete transaction functionality with confirmation dialog on the Transaction History page.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention the ability to delete transactions. The TransactionHistoryPage implements a delete button with AlertDialog confirmation and calls actor.deleteTransaction().

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity and authentication integration wrapping the entire application.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention Internet Identity, authentication, or ICP-specific identity management. The application wraps everything in InternetIdentityProvider and creates authenticated actors.
